title: assembly-panel
content:
Assembly Panel

The Assembly panel (titled Assembly Workflow) is used to define and play back step-by-step assembly or disassembly sequences with smooth GSAP animations. It has two main sub-sections: Playback and Edit.

Playback

Controls for navigating and animating the assembly sequence.

  • Status – Read-only label showing the current playback position (e.g. Step 2 / 5 – Attach lid).

  • Loop ∞ (start ↔ finish) – When checked, the animation loops continuously between the start and finish positions.

  • Camera 🎥 (animate camera) – When checked, the camera moves to the saved view for each step during animated playback.

  • Zoom coeff 🔍 (ortho only) – Scaling coefficient (0.1–5.0) applied to the orthographic camera zoom during camera animation. Has no effect in perspective mode.

  • ⏮ Reset to start [Home] – Instantly moves all objects to their fully assembled position (before step 1). Keyboard shortcut: Home.

  • ◀◀ Animate to start [Shift+PgUp] – Plays the animation backward to the start position. Keyboard shortcut: Shift+PageUp.

  • ◀ Previous step [PageUp] – Steps one step backward. Keyboard shortcut: PageUp.

  • Next step ▶ [PageDown] – Steps one step forward. Keyboard shortcut: PageDown.

  • Animate to finish ▶▶ [Shift+PgDn] – Plays the animation forward to the last step. Keyboard shortcut: Shift+PageDown.

  • Reset to finish ⏭ [End] – Instantly moves all objects to their fully disassembled position (after the last step). Keyboard shortcut: End.

Animation Settings

Fine-grained GSAP tween parameters applied to all step animations.

  • Duration (ms) – Length of each step animation in milliseconds (0–2000).

  • Ease – GSAP easing function applied to every object movement. Options include none, power1–4, back, bounce, elastic, circ, expo, sine, each with .in, .out, and .inOut variants.

  • Repeat (-1=∞) – Number of times each step animation repeats. -1 = infinite.

  • Delay (ms) – Pause before each animation starts (0–2000 ms).

  • Repeat delay (ms) – Pause between repetitions (0–2000 ms).

  • Yoyo – When checked, each repeat plays the animation in reverse (forward → back → forward…).

  • Stagger (ms) – Time offset between individual object animations within the same step (0–1000 ms). Creates a cascading effect.

  • Overwrite – GSAP overwrite mode: auto (smart conflict resolution), true (kill all previous tweens on target), or false (no overwrite).

Edit

Tools for creating and modifying assembly steps. All controls except Edit mode are disabled until edit mode is activated.

  • Edit mode – Enables assembly editing. When active, moving a named object in the viewport records its new position as a transformation for the currently active step.

  • Active step – Read-only label indicating which step is currently being edited.

  • Step name – Editable name for the active step, shown in the Status label during playback.

  • Description – Optional free-text description for the active step.

  • + New step – Creates a new empty step after the current position.

  • ✕ Delete step – Removes the currently active step and all its recorded transformations.

  • ↑ Move step up – Moves the active step one position earlier in the sequence.

  • ↓ Move step down – Moves the active step one position later in the sequence.

  • ✕ Remove object from step – Removes the currently selected object's transformation from the active step (the object will no longer move during that step).

  • 📷 Save camera view – Stores the current camera position and orientation as the camera view for the active step. Used during animated playback when Camera is enabled.

  • ✕ Clear camera view – Removes the saved camera view from the active step.

Workflow: Creating an Assembly

  1. Load the fully assembled model.

  2. Open the Assembly panel and switch to Edit mode.

  3. Click + New step. Give the step a name.

  4. Select an object and drag it to its disassembled position. The transformation is recorded automatically.

  5. Repeat steps 3–4 for each assembly step.

  6. Use the Playback controls to review the animation.

  7. Save the model via the File panel to embed the assembly data.
